---
title: "plotting functions"
author: "Andrea Chen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
# ---- Multi-filter time-series plot (base R) ----
# df columns expected: tick (time), rep (replicate), plus parameters (e.g., reg, n, psi, shift_size, ...)
# You choose which numeric column to plot on y via ycol.
#
# Arguments:
#   df        : data.frame
#   filters   : list of named lists, each like list(reg="ave", n=1000, psi=0.5, shift_size=200)
#               Values can be length >1 vectors for OR-matching, e.g., psi=c(0,0.5)
#   ycol      : character name of the numeric column to plot (e.g., "psi_mean", "seg_site", etc.)
#   labels    : list(title=..., x=..., y=...)
#   show_rep  : logical; if TRUE, plot faint per-rep lines in background
#   cols      : vector of colors, one per filter
#   xlim, ylim: numeric length-2; optional axis limits
#   tick_col  : name of the time column (default "tick")
#   lwd_mean  : line width for averaged line
#   lwd_rep   : line width for replicate lines
#   rep_alpha : alpha for replicate lines (0..1)
#
plot_lines_multi <- function(
  df,
  filters,
  ycol,
  labels = list(title = NULL, x = "Tick", y = ycol),
  show_rep = TRUE,
  cols = NULL,
  xlim = NULL,
  ylim = NULL,
  tick_col = "tick",
  lwd_mean = 3,
  lwd_rep  = 1,
  rep_alpha = 0.35
) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df), length(filters) >= 1, is.character(ycol), ycol %in% names(df))
  if (!tick_col %in% names(df)) stop("tick column '", tick_col, "' not found.")
  if (is.null(cols)) cols <- rep("black", length(filters))
  if (length(cols) < length(filters)) {
    cols <- rep(cols, length.out = length(filters))
  }

  # helper: apply one filter list to df (AND across named fields; OR within a vector value)
  apply_filter <- function(d, f) {
    keep <- rep(TRUE, nrow(d))
    for (nm in names(f)) {
      if (!nm %in% names(d)) next
      v <- f[[nm]]
      keep <- keep & (d[[nm]] %in% v)
    }
    d[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  }

  # prepare objects for ranges
  all_ticks <- numeric(0)
  all_vals  <- numeric(0)

  # precompute per-filter summaries
  summaries <- lapply(seq_along(filters), function(i) {
    f <- filters[[i]]
    d <- apply_filter(df, f)
    # coerce types we need
    d[[tick_col]] <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(d[[tick_col]]))
    d$rep         <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(d$rep))
    d[[ycol]]     <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d[[ycol]]))
    d <- d[is.finite(d[[tick_col]]) & is.finite(d$rep) & is.finite(d[[ycol]]), , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) {
      return(list(reps = list(), mean = data.frame(tick = integer(0), y = numeric(0))))
    }

    # split by rep for optional background
    by_rep <- split(d[, c(tick_col, ycol)], d$rep)
    by_rep <- lapply(by_rep, function(x) {
      o <- order(x[[tick_col]])
      data.frame(tick = x[[tick_col]][o], y = x[[ycol]][o])
    })

    # across-rep mean per tick
    # aggregate mean(y | tick)
    mean_df <- aggregate(d[[ycol]], by = list(tick = d[[tick_col]]), FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
    names(mean_df)[2] <- "y"
    mean_df <- mean_df[order(mean_df$tick), ]

    all_ticks <<- c(all_ticks, mean_df$tick)
    all_vals  <<- c(all_vals,  mean_df$y)

    list(reps = by_rep, mean = mean_df)
  })

  # set up plot region
  if (is.null(xlim)) xlim <- range(all_ticks, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.null(ylim)) ylim <- range(all_vals,  na.rm = TRUE)

  plot(NA, NA, xlim = xlim, ylim = ylim, xlab = labels$x, ylab = labels$y, main = labels$title)

  # draw, filter by filter
  for (i in seq_along(summaries)) {
    col_i <- cols[i]
    s <- summaries[[i]]

    # background replicate lines
    if (show_rep && length(s$reps)) {
      col_rep <- tryCatch(adjustcolor(col_i, alpha.f = rep_alpha), error = function(e) col_i)
      for (rp in s$reps) {
        if (nrow(rp) > 1) points(rp$tick, rp$y, col = col_rep, lwd = lwd_rep, pch = 16)
      }
    }
    # averaged line
    if (nrow(s$mean) > 1) lines(s$mean$tick, s$mean$y, col = col_i, lwd = lwd_mean)
  }

  invisible(NULL)
}

```
